claims.claim: We investigate an ordering-only integration strategy for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) prompt construction, termed *evidence-first answerability cutoff*, which reorders retrieved chunks so that evidence-bearing passages precede metadata and context-only passages within a fixed token budget.

claims.claim: In retrieval-augmented generation systems, the prompt construction strategy—how retrieved passages are selected, ordered, and truncated to fit a token budget—directly affects both generation quality and inference cost.
